    79→// WatchForProjectDir watches for a project directory that matches the current working directory
    80→func WatchForProjectDir() error {
    81→	slog.Info("WatchForProjectDir: Determining project directory to monitor")
    82→	claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir("")
    83→	if err != nil {
    84→		slog.Error("WatchForProjectDir: Failed to get project directory", "error", err)
    85→		// Check if error is specifically about missing Claude projects directory
    86→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "claude projects directory not found") {
    87→			slog.Error("WatchForProjectDir: Claude projects directory not found while starting setup watcher")
    88→			// Start watching for Claude directory creation
    89→			return WatchForClaudeSetup()
    90→		}
    91→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get project directory: %v", err)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	slog.Info("WatchForProjectDir: Project directory found", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
    95→	return startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir)
    96→}
    97→
    98→// startProjectWatcher starts watching a specific project directory
    99→func startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir string) error {
   100→	slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Creating file watcher", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   101→
   102→	// Create a new watcher
   103→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Increment wait group before starting goroutine
   109→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   110→
   111→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   112→	go func() {
   113→		// Decrement wait group when done
   114→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   115→
   116→		// Log when goroutine starts
   117→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine started")
   118→
   119→		// Defer cleanup
   120→		defer func() {
   121→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine exiting, closing watcher")
   122→			_ = watcher.Close() // Best effort cleanup; errors here are not recoverable
   123→		}()
   124→
   125→		// Check if project directory already exists
   126→		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); err == nil {
   127→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Project directory exists", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   128→			// Claude Code triggers file system events when it starts writing, so we'll catch the session on the first write event.
   129→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Waiting for JSONL file system events")
   130→		} else {
   131→			// Project directory doesn't exist yet, watch parent directory (or other error)
   132→			parentDir := filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)
   133→			projectName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   134→			slog.Warn("startProjectWatcher: Cannot access project directory, watching parent",
   135→				"error", err,
   136→				"parentDir", parentDir,
   137→				"projectName", projectName)
   138→
   139→			// Add parent directory to watcher
   140→			if err := watcher.Add(parentDir); err != nil {
   141→				log.UserError("Error watching parent directory: %v", err)
   142→				slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to watch parent directory", "error", err)
   143→				return
   144→			}
   145→
   146→			// Wait for project directory to be created
   147→			waitingForCreation := true
   148→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now watching for events in parent directory")
   149→			for waitingForCreation {
   150→				select {
   151→				case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   152→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled while waiting for directory creation")
   153→					return
   154→				case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   155→					if !ok {
   156→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher events channel closed")
   157→						return
   158→					}
   159→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Parent directory event",
   160→						"operation", event.Op.String(),
   161→						"path", event.Name,
   162→						"lookingFor", projectName)
   163→					eventBaseName := filepath.Base(event.Name)
   164→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Event basename comparison",
   165→						"basename", eventBaseName,
   166→						"comparingWith", projectName,
   167→						"equalFold", strings.EqualFold(eventBaseName, projectName))
   168→					if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), projectName) {
   169→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Detected project directory creation",
   170→							"path", event.Name)
   171→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Initial scan disabled; waiting for JSONL events")
   172→						// Use the actual created directory name from the event
   173→						claudeProjectDir = filepath.Join(parentDir, filepath.Base(event.Name))
   174→						// Remove parent directory and continue to watch project directory
   175→						_ = watcher.Remove(parentDir) // OK if this fails; we're moving to project dir
   176→						waitingForCreation = false
   177→					}
   178→				case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   179→					if !ok {
   180→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher errors channel closed")
   181→						return
   182→					}
   183→					log.UserWarn("Watcher error while waiting for project directory: %v", err)
   184→					slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Watcher error", "error", err)
   185→				}
   186→			}
   187→		}
   188→
   189→		// Add the project directory to the watcher
   190→		// Re-stat to get the actual directory name with correct case
   191→		actualProjectDir := claudeProjectDir
   192→		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)); err == nil {
   193→			baseName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   194→			for _, entry := range entries {
   195→				if strings.EqualFold(entry.Name(), baseName) {
   196→					actualProjectDir = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir), entry.Name())
   197→					break
   198→				}
   199→			}
   200→		}
   201→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now monitoring project directory for changes",
   202→			"directory", actualProjectDir)
   203→		if err := watcher.Add(actualProjectDir); err != nil {
   204→			log.UserWarn("Error watching project directory: %v", err)
   205→			slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to monitor directory", "error", err)
   206→			return
   207→		}
   208→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Successfully monitoring project directory for future changes")
   209→
   210→		// Watch for file events
   211→		for {
   212→			select {
   213→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   214→				slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   215→				return
   216→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   217→				if !ok {
   218→					return
   219→				}
   220→
   221→				// Only process JSONL files
   222→				if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".jsonl") {
   223→					continue
   224→				}
   225→
   226→				if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
   227→					continue
   228→				}
   229→
   230→				// Handle different event types
   231→				switch {
   232→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Create):
   233→					slog.Info("New JSONL file created", "file", event.Name)
   234→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   235→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Write):
   236→					slog.Info("JSONL file modified", "file", event.Name)
   237→					slog.Info("Triggering scan due to file modification")
   238→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   239→				}
   240→
   241→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   242→				if !ok {
   243→					return
   244→				}
   245→				log.UserError("Watcher error: %v", err)
   246→			}
   247→		}
   248→	}()
   249→
   250→	return nil
   251→}
   252→
   253→// scanJSONLFiles scans JSONL files and optionally filters processing to a specific changed file
   254→func scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir string, changedFile ...string) {
   255→	// Ensure logs are flushed even if we panic
   256→	defer func() {
   257→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
   258→			log.UserWarn("PANIC in ScanJSONLFiles: %v", r)
   259→			slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: PANIC recovered", "panic", r)
   260→		}
   261→	}()
   262→
   263→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === START SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   264→	var targetFile string
   265→	if len(changedFile) > 0 && changedFile[0] != "" {
   266→		targetFile = changedFile[0]
   267→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files with changed file",
   268→			"directory", claudeProjectDir,
   269→			"changedFile", targetFile)
   270→	} else {
   271→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   272→	}
   273→	// Parse files and create history files:
   274→	parser := NewJSONLParser()
   275→	var targetSessionUuid string
   276→
   277→	// Set the target file for conditional debug output
   278→	if targetFile != "" {
   279→		SetDebugTargetFile(targetFile)
   280→		defer ClearDebugTargetFile()
   281→
   282→		sessionID, err := extractSessionIDFromFile(targetFile)
   283→		if err != nil {
   284→			slog.Warn("ScanJSONLFiles: Failed to extract session ID from file",
   285→				"file", targetFile,
   286→				"error", err)
   287→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan due to file read error", "file", targetFile)
   288→			return
   289→		}
   290→		if sessionID == "" {
   291→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan; session ID not present yet", "file", targetFile)
   292→			return
   293→		}
   294→		targetSessionUuid = sessionID
   295→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Extracted session ID from changed file",
   296→			"sessionUuid", targetSessionUuid,
   297→			"file", targetFile)
   298→	}
   299→
   300→	var parseErr error
   301→	if targetSessionUuid != "" {
   302→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessionsForSession(claudeProjectDir, true, targetSessionUuid)
   303→	} else {
   304→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessions(claudeProjectDir, true)
   305→	}
   306→	if parseErr != nil {
   307→		log.UserWarn("Error parsing project sessions: %v", parseErr)
   308→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Error parsing project sessions", "error", parseErr)
   309→		return
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Process JSONL sessions (isAutosave = true, silent = false for autosave)
   313→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions",
   314→		"sessionCount", len(parser.Sessions),
   315→		"targetSessionUuid", targetSessionUuid)
   316→
   317→	// Get the session callback
   318→	callback := getWatcherCallback()
   319→	if callback == nil {
   320→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: No callback provided - this should not happen")
   321→		return
   322→	}
   323→
   324→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions with callback")
   325→	// Process sessions and call the callback for each
   326→	for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
   327→		// Skip empty sessions
   328→		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   329→			continue
   330→		}
   331→
   332→		// Skip if we're targeting a specific session and this isn't it
   333→		if targetSessionUuid != "" && session.SessionUuid != targetSessionUuid {
   334→			continue
   335→		}
   336→
   337→		// Convert to AgentChatSession (workspaceRoot extracted from records' cwd field)
   338→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, "", getWatcherDebugRaw())
   339→		if agentSession != nil {
   340→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Calling callback for session", "sessionId", agentSession.SessionID)
   341→			// Call the callback in a goroutine to avoid blocking
   342→			go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   343→				defer func() {
   344→					if r := recover(); r != nil {
   345→						slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Callback panicked", "panic", r)
   346→					}
   347→				}()
   348→				callback(s)
   349→			}(agentSession)
   350→		}
   351→	}
   352→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing complete")
   353→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === END SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   354→}
   355→
   356→// WatchForClaudeSetup watches for the creation of Claude directories
   357→func WatchForClaudeSetup() error {
   358→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Starting Claude directory setup watcher")
   359→	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	claudeDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude")
   365→	projectsDir := filepath.Join(claudeDir, "projects")
   366→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Directories",
   367→		"homeDir", homeDir,
   368→		"claudeDir", claudeDir,
   369→		"projectsDir", projectsDir)
   370→
   371→	// Determine what to watch
   372→	var watchDir string
   373→	var watchingFor string
   374→
   375→	if _, err := os.Stat(claudeDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   376→		// .claude doesn't exist, watch home directory
   377→		watchDir = homeDir
   378→		watchingFor = ".claude"
   379→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory does not exist")
   380→		slog.Info("Claude directory not found, watching for creation of ~/.claude\n")
   381→	} else {
   382→		// .claude exists but projects doesn't, watch .claude
   383→		watchDir = claudeDir
   384→		watchingFor = "projects"
   385→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory exists, checking for projects")
   386→		slog.Info("Claude directory found, watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   387→	}
   388→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Will watch directory",
   389→		"watchDir", watchDir,
   390→		"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   391→
   392→	// Create watcher
   393→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   394→	if err != nil {
   395→		log.UserWarn("Failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   396→		slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to create file watcher", "error", err)
   397→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	// Add directory to watch
   401→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Adding directory to watcher", "directory", watchDir)
   402→	if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   403→		_ = watcher.Close() // Clean up on error; close errors less important than Add error
   404→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to watch directory %s: %v", watchDir, err)
   405→	}
   406→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started watching", "directory", watchDir)
   407→
   408→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   409→	go func() {
   410→		defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }() // Cleanup on exit; errors not recoverable
   411→
   412→		for {
   413→			select {
   414→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   415→				if !ok {
   416→					return
   417→				}
   418→
   419→				slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Received event",
   420→					"operation", event.Op.String(),
   421→					"path", event.Name)
   422→				// Check if this is the directory we're waiting for (case-insensitive)
   423→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), watchingFor) {
   424→					slog.Info("Detected creation", "path", event.Name)
   425→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Target directory created", "path", event.Name)
   426→
   427→					// Wait a second to let things settle
   428→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Waiting 1 second for things to settle")
   429→					time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
   430→
   431→					// Check what we should do next
   432→					if watchingFor == ".claude" {
   433→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude was created, checking if projects exists")
   434→						// We were watching for .claude, now check if projects exists
   435→						if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   436→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory does not exist, switching watcher")
   437→							// Need to watch for projects directory
   438→							_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; switching watch targets
   439→							watchDir = claudeDir
   440→							watchingFor = "projects"
   441→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Switching to watch",
   442→								"watchDir", watchDir,
   443→								"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   444→							if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   445→								log.UserWarn("Error switching to watch .claude directory: %v", err)
   446→								slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to switch watcher", "error", err)
   447→								return
   448→							}
   449→							slog.Info("Now watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   450→							continue
   451→						} else {
   452→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory already exists!")
   453→						}
   454→					}
   455→
   456→					// If we get here, projects directory should exist
   457→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Projects directory should now exist, cleaning up watcher")
   458→					// Remove the current watcher
   459→					_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; we're transitioning to project watcher
   460→
   461→					// Start the normal project watcher
   462→					slog.Info("Claude projects directory now exists, starting normal file watcher\n")
   463→					// Try to get the project directory again
   464→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Getting project directory name")
   465→					if projectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(""); err == nil {
   466→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Project directory will be", "directory", projectDir)
   467→						if err := startProjectWatcher(projectDir); err != nil {
   468→							log.UserWarn("Error starting project watcher: %v", err)
   469→							slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to start project watcher", "error", err)
   470→						} else {
   471→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started project watcher")
   472→						}
   473→					} else {
   474→						log.UserWarn("Error getting project directory after Claude setup: %v", err)
   475→						slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to get project directory", "error", err)
   476→					}
   477→					return
   478→				}
   479→
   480→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   481→				if !ok {
   482→					return
   483→				}
   484→				log.UserWarn("Watcher error: %v", err)
   485→				slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Watcher error", "error", err)
   486→			}
   487→		}
   488→	}()
   489→
   490→	return nil
   491→}
   492→
   493→// convertToAgentChatSession converts a Session to an AgentChatSession
   494→// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
   495→func convertToAgentChatSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
   496→	// Skip empty sessions (no records)
   497→	if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   498→		return nil
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	// Write debug files first (even for warmup-only sessions)
   502→	_ = make(map[int]int) // recordToFileNumber no longer needed
   503→	if debugRaw {
   504→		// Write debug files
   505→		writeDebugRawFiles(session)
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	// Filter warmup messages
   509→	filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
   510→
   511→	// Skip if no real messages remain after filtering warmup
   512→	if len(filteredRecords) == 0 {
   513→		slog.Debug("Skipping warmup-only session", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid)
   514→		return nil
   515→	}
   516→
   517→	// Create session with filtered records
   518→	filteredSession := Session{
   519→		SessionUuid: session.SessionUuid,
   520→		Records:     filteredRecords,
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	// Get timestamp from root record
   524→	rootRecord := filteredSession.Records[0]
   525→	timestamp, ok := rootRecord.Data["timestamp"].(string)
   526→	if !ok {
   527→		slog.Warn("convertToAgentChatSession: No timestamp found in root record")
   528→		return nil
   529→	}
   530→
   531→	// Get the slug
   532→	slug := FileSlugFromRootRecord(filteredSession)
   533→
   534→	// Generate SessionData from filtered session
   535→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(filteredSession, workspaceRoot)
   536→	if err != nil {
   537→		slog.Error("Failed to generate SessionData", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid, "error", err)
   538→		return nil
   539→	}
   540→
   541→	// Convert session records to JSONL format for raw data
   542→	// Note: Uses unfiltered session.Records (not filteredSession.Records) to preserve
   543→	// all records including warmup messages in the raw data for complete audit trail
   544→	var rawDataBuilder strings.Builder
   545→	for _, record := range session.Records {
   546→		jsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(record.Data)
   547→		rawDataBuilder.Write(jsonBytes)
   548→		rawDataBuilder.WriteString("\n")
   549→	}
   550→
   551→	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
   552→		SessionID:   session.SessionUuid,
   553→		CreatedAt:   timestamp, // ISO 8601 timestamp
   554→		Slug:        slug,
   555→		SessionData: sessionData,
   556→		RawData:     rawDataBuilder.String(),
   557→	}
   558→}
   559→
```

    15→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"

    18→var (

    20→	watcherCancel   context.CancelFunc
    21→	watcherWg       sync.WaitGroup
    22→	watcherCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession) // Callback for session updates
    23→	watcherDebugRaw bool                        // Whether to write debug raw data files

    27→func init() {

    30→
    31→// SetWatcherCallback sets the callback function for session updates.
    32→func SetWatcherCallback(callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) {
    33→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    34→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    35→	watcherCallback = callback
    36→	slog.Info("SetWatcherCallback: Callback set", "isNil", callback == nil)
    37→}
    38→
    39→// ClearWatcherCallback clears the callback function.
    40→func ClearWatcherCallback() {
    41→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    42→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    43→	watcherCallback = nil
    44→	slog.Info("ClearWatcherCallback: Callback cleared")
    45→}
    46→
    47→// getWatcherCallback returns the current callback function (thread-safe).
    48→func getWatcherCallback() func(*spi.AgentChatSession) {
    49→	watcherMutex.RLock()
    50→	defer watcherMutex.RUnlock()
    51→	return watcherCallback
    52→}
    53→

    61→

    78→// WatchForCodexSessions watches for Codex CLI sessions that match the given project path.
    79→// If resumeSessionID is provided, it finds and watches the directory containing that session.
    80→// Otherwise, watches hierarchically for new sessions, handling date changes across days/months/years.
    81→func WatchForCodexSessions(projectPath string, resumeSessionID string) error {
    82→	slog.Info("WatchForCodexSessions: Starting Codex session watcher",
    83→		"projectPath", projectPath,
    84→		"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
    85→
    86→	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		slog.Error("WatchForCodexSessions: Failed to get home directory", "error", err)
    89→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get home directory: %w", err)
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
    93→	slog.Info("WatchForCodexSessions: Sessions root", "path", sessionsRoot)
    94→
    95→	var initialDayDir string
    96→
    97→	if resumeSessionID != "" {
    98→		// Find the directory containing the resumed session
    99→		slog.Info("WatchForCodexSessions: Finding directory for resumed session", "sessionID", resumeSessionID)
   100→
   101→		// Use findCodexSessions to locate the specific session (will short-circuit when found)
   102→		sessions, err := findCodexSessions(projectPath, resumeSessionID, false)
   103→		if err != nil {
   104→			slog.Error("WatchForCodexSessions: Failed to find resumed session", "error", err)
   105→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to find resumed session: %w", err)
   106→		}
   107→
   108→		// Check if session was found
   109→		if len(sessions) == 0 {
   110→			slog.Error("WatchForCodexSessions: Resumed session not found", "sessionID", resumeSessionID)
   111→			return fmt.Errorf("resumed session %s not found", resumeSessionID)
   112→		}
   113→
   114→		// Get the directory containing the session file
   115→		initialDayDir = filepath.Dir(sessions[0].SessionPath)
   116→		slog.Info("WatchForCodexSessions: Found resumed session directory", "path", initialDayDir)
   117→	} else {
   118→		// Calculate today's directory (will be watched along with hierarchical watching)
   119→		now := time.Now()
   120→		initialDayDir = filepath.Join(sessionsRoot, fmt.Sprintf("%04d", now.Year()), fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Month()), fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Day()))
   121→		slog.Info("WatchForCodexSessions: Initial day directory", "path", initialDayDir)
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	return startCodexSessionWatcher(projectPath, sessionsRoot, initialDayDir)
   125→}
   126→
   127→// dirType determines the type of directory relative to sessionsRoot based on the
   128→// YYYY/MM/DD structure. Returns "year", "month", "day", or "" if not a recognized type.
   129→func dirType(path string, sessionsRoot string) string {
   130→	rel, err := filepath.Rel(sessionsRoot, path)
   131→	if err != nil || strings.HasPrefix(rel, "..") {
   132→		return "" // Path is not under sessionsRoot
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	parts := strings.Split(filepath.ToSlash(rel), "/")
   136→	switch len(parts) {
   137→	case 1: // YYYY
   138→		if len(parts[0]) == 4 {
   139→			return "year"
   140→		}
   141→	case 2: // YYYY/MM
   142→		if len(parts[0]) == 4 && len(parts[1]) == 2 {
   143→			return "month"
   144→		}
   145→	case 3: // YYYY/MM/DD
   146→		if len(parts[0]) == 4 && len(parts[1]) == 2 && len(parts[2]) == 2 {
   147→			return "day"
   148→		}
   149→	}
   150→	return ""
   151→}
   152→
   153→// startCodexSessionWatcher starts watching hierarchically for Codex sessions.
   154→// Watches sessionsRoot/YYYY/MM/DD/ structure to handle date changes across days, months, and years.
   155→// The initialDayDir is scanned immediately if it exists.
   156→func startCodexSessionWatcher(projectPath string, sessionsRoot string, initialDayDir string) error {
   157→	slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Creating hierarchical watcher",
   158→		"sessionsRoot", sessionsRoot,
   159→		"initialDayDir", initialDayDir)
   160→
   161→	// Create a new watcher
   162→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   163→	if err != nil {
   164→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   165→	}
   166→
   167→	// Increment wait group before starting goroutine
   168→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   169→
   170→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   171→	go func() {
   172→		// Decrement wait group when done
   173→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   174→
   175→		// Log when goroutine starts
   176→		slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Goroutine started")
   177→
   178→		// Defer cleanup
   179→		defer func() {
   180→			slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Goroutine exiting, closing watcher")
   181→			if err := watcher.Close(); err != nil {
   182→				slog.Debug("startCodexSessionWatcher: Error closing watcher", "error", err)
   183→			}
   184→		}()
   185→
   186→		// Track which directories are being watched to avoid duplicates
   187→		watchedDirs := make(map[string]bool)
   188→		var watchedDirsMutex sync.Mutex
   189→
   190→		// Helper to add a directory to watcher if not already watched
   191→		addWatch := func(dir string) error {
   192→			watchedDirsMutex.Lock()
   193→			defer watchedDirsMutex.Unlock()
   194→
   195→			if watchedDirs[dir] {
   196→				return nil // Already watching
   197→			}
   198→
   199→			if err := watcher.Add(dir); err != nil {
   200→				return err
   201→			}
   202→			watchedDirs[dir] = true
   203→			slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Added watch", "directory", dir)
   204→			return nil
   205→		}
   206→
   207→		// Helper to scan a day directory for existing sessions
   208→		scanDayDir := func(dayDir string) {
   209→			if _, err := os.Stat(dayDir); err == nil {
   210→				slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Scanning day directory", "directory", dayDir)
   211→				ScanCodexSessions(projectPath, dayDir, nil)
   212→			}
   213→		}
   214→
   215→		// Helper to watch a day directory and scan it
   216→		watchDayDir := func(dayDir string) {
   217→			if err := addWatch(dayDir); err != nil {
   218→				slog.Error("startCodexSessionWatcher: Failed to watch day directory",
   219→					"directory", dayDir,
   220→					"error", err)
   221→				return
   222→			}
   223→			scanDayDir(dayDir)
   224→		}
   225→
   226→		// Helper to watch a month directory and its existing day directories
   227→		watchMonthDir := func(monthDir string) {
   228→			if err := addWatch(monthDir); err != nil {
   229→				slog.Error("startCodexSessionWatcher: Failed to watch month directory",
   230→					"directory", monthDir,
   231→					"error", err)
   232→				return
   233→			}
   234→
   235→			// Scan for existing day directories
   236→			entries, err := os.ReadDir(monthDir)
   237→			if err != nil {
   238→				slog.Debug("startCodexSessionWatcher: Cannot read month directory",
   239→					"directory", monthDir,
   240→					"error", err)
   241→				return
   242→			}
   243→
   244→			for _, entry := range entries {
   245→				if !entry.IsDir() {
   246→					continue
   247→				}
   248→				// Day directories are named 01-31
   249→				if len(entry.Name()) == 2 {
   250→					dayDir := filepath.Join(monthDir, entry.Name())
   251→					watchDayDir(dayDir)
   252→				}
   253→			}
   254→		}
   255→
   256→		// Helper to watch a year directory and its existing month directories
   257→		watchYearDir := func(yearDir string) {
   258→			if err := addWatch(yearDir); err != nil {
   259→				slog.Error("startCodexSessionWatcher: Failed to watch year directory",
   260→					"directory", yearDir,
   261→					"error", err)
   262→				return
   263→			}
   264→
   265→			// Scan for existing month directories
   266→			entries, err := os.ReadDir(yearDir)
   267→			if err != nil {
   268→				slog.Debug("startCodexSessionWatcher: Cannot read year directory",
   269→					"directory", yearDir,
   270→					"error", err)
   271→				return
   272→			}
   273→
   274→			for _, entry := range entries {
   275→				if !entry.IsDir() {
   276→					continue
   277→				}
   278→				// Month directories are named 01-12
   279→				if len(entry.Name()) == 2 {
   280→					monthDir := filepath.Join(yearDir, entry.Name())
   281→					watchMonthDir(monthDir)
   282→				}
   283→			}
   284→		}
   285→
   286→		// Watch sessions root for year directories
   287→		if err := addWatch(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
   288→			log.UserError("Error watching sessions root: %v", err)
   289→			slog.Error("startCodexSessionWatcher: Failed to watch sessions root", "error", err)
   290→			return
   291→		}
   292→
   293→		// Scan for existing year directories
   294→		entries, err := os.ReadDir(sessionsRoot)
   295→		if err != nil {
   296→			slog.Warn("startCodexSessionWatcher: Cannot read sessions root",
   297→				"directory", sessionsRoot,
   298→				"error", err)
   299→		} else {
   300→			for _, entry := range entries {
   301→				if !entry.IsDir() {
   302→					continue
   303→				}
   304→				// Year directories are named YYYY (4 digits)
   305→				if len(entry.Name()) == 4 {
   306→					yearDir := filepath.Join(sessionsRoot, entry.Name())
   307→					watchYearDir(yearDir)
   308→				}
   309→			}
   310→		}
   311→
   312→		// Scan the initial day directory if it exists
   313→		scanDayDir(initialDayDir)
   314→
   315→		// Watch for events
   316→		slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Now watching for file and directory events")
   317→		for {
   318→			select {
   319→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   320→				slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   321→				return
   322→
   323→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   324→				if !ok {
   325→					slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Watcher events channel closed")
   326→					return
   327→				}
   328→
   329→				if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Remove) {
   330→					continue
   331→				}
   332→
   333→				// Determine what type of path this is
   334→				eventPath := event.Name
   335→				parentDir := filepath.Dir(eventPath)
   336→
   337→				// Check if this is a JSONL file (in a day directory)
   338→				if strings.HasSuffix(eventPath, ".jsonl") {
   339→					switch {
   340→					case event.Has(fsnotify.Create), event.Has(fsnotify.Write):
   341→						slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: JSONL file event",
   342→							"operation", event.Op.String(),
   343→							"file", eventPath)
   344→						ScanCodexSessions(projectPath, parentDir, &eventPath)
   345→					case event.Has(fsnotify.Remove):
   346→						slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: JSONL file removed", "file", eventPath)
   347→						ScanCodexSessions(projectPath, parentDir, nil)
   348→					}
   349→					continue
   350→				}
   351→
   352→				// Check if this is a directory creation
   353→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   354→					switch dirType(eventPath, sessionsRoot) {
   355→					case "year":
   356→						slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: New year directory created", "directory", eventPath)
   357→						watchYearDir(eventPath)
   358→					case "month":
   359→						slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: New month directory created", "directory", eventPath)
   360→						watchMonthDir(eventPath)
   361→					case "day":
   362→						slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: New day directory created", "directory", eventPath)
   363→						watchDayDir(eventPath)
   364→					}
   365→				}
   366→
   367→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   368→				if !ok {
   369→					slog.Info("startCodexSessionWatcher: Watcher errors channel closed")
   370→					return
   371→				}
   372→				log.UserWarn("Watcher error: %v", err)
   373→				slog.Error("startCodexSessionWatcher: Watcher error", "error", err)
   374→			}
   375→		}
   376→	}()
   377→
   378→	return nil
   379→}
   380→
   381→// ScanCodexSessions scans JSONL files in the session directory and processes sessions
   382→// that match the project path. If changedFile is nil, scans all JSONL files in the directory.
   383→// If changedFile is non-nil, only processes that specific file.
   384→func ScanCodexSessions(projectPath string, sessionDir string, changedFile *string) {
   385→	// Ensure logs are flushed even if we panic
   386→	defer func() {
   387→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
   388→			log.UserWarn("PANIC in ScanCodexSessions: %v", r)
   389→			slog.Error("ScanCodexSessions: PANIC recovered", "panic", r)
   390→		}
   391→	}()
   392→
   393→	slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: === START SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   394→	if changedFile != nil {
   395→		slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: Scanning with changed file",
   396→			"projectPath", projectPath,
   397→			"sessionDir", sessionDir,
   398→			"changedFile", *changedFile)
   399→	} else {
   400→		slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: Scanning all sessions",
   401→			"projectPath", projectPath,
   402→			"sessionDir", sessionDir)
   403→	}
   404→
   405→	// Get the session callback
   406→	callback := getWatcherCallback()
   407→	if callback == nil {
   408→		slog.Error("ScanCodexSessions: No callback provided - this should not happen")
   409→		return
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	// Normalize project path for comparison
   413→	normalizedProjectPath := normalizeCodexPath(projectPath)
   414→	if normalizedProjectPath == "" {
   415→		slog.Debug("ScanCodexSessions: Unable to normalize project path", "projectPath", projectPath)
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	// If we have a specific changed file, only process that file
   419→	if changedFile != nil {
   420→		if err := processCodexSessionFile(*changedFile, projectPath, normalizedProjectPath, callback); err != nil {
   421→			slog.Debug("ScanCodexSessions: Failed to process changed file",
   422→				"file", *changedFile,
   423→				"error", err)
   424→		}
   425→		slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: === END SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   426→		return
   427→	}
   428→
   429→	// Otherwise, scan all JSONL files in the session directory
   430→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(sessionDir)
   431→	if err != nil {
   432→		slog.Error("ScanCodexSessions: Failed to read session directory",
   433→			"directory", sessionDir,
   434→			"error", err)
   435→		return
   436→	}
   437→
   438→	for _, entry := range entries {
   439→		if entry.IsDir() {
   440→			continue
   441→		}
   442→		if filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) != ".jsonl" {
   443→			continue
   444→		}
   445→
   446→		sessionPath := filepath.Join(sessionDir, entry.Name())
   447→		if err := processCodexSessionFile(sessionPath, projectPath, normalizedProjectPath, callback); err != nil {
   448→			slog.Debug("ScanCodexSessions: Failed to process session file",
   449→				"file", sessionPath,
   450→				"error", err)
   451→			// Continue processing other files even if one fails
   452→		}
   453→	}
   454→
   455→	slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: Processing complete")
   456→	slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: === END SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   457→}
   458→
   459→// processCodexSessionFile processes a single Codex session file and calls the callback
   460→// if the session matches the project path.
   461→func processCodexSessionFile(sessionPath string, projectPath string, normalizedProjectPath string, callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   462→	// Load session metadata
   463→	meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(sessionPath)
   464→	if err != nil {
   465→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session meta: %w", err)
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	// Check if session matches project path
   469→	sessionID := strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
   470→	normalizedCWD := normalizeCodexPath(meta.Payload.CWD)
   471→	if normalizedCWD == "" {
   472→		slog.Debug("processCodexSessionFile: Session meta missing cwd", "sessionID", sessionID, "path", sessionPath)
   473→		return fmt.Errorf("session meta missing cwd")
   474→	}
   475→
   476→	// Check if this session matches the project path
   477→	matched := false
   478→	if normalizedProjectPath != "" {
   479→		if normalizedCWD == normalizedProjectPath || strings.EqualFold(normalizedCWD, normalizedProjectPath) {
   480→			matched = true
   481→		}
   482→	} else if normalizedCWD == projectPath || strings.EqualFold(normalizedCWD, projectPath) {
   483→		matched = true
   484→	}
   485→
   486→	if !matched {
   487→		slog.Debug("processCodexSessionFile: Session does not match project path",
   488→			"sessionID", sessionID,
   489→			"sessionCWD", normalizedCWD,
   490→			"projectPath", normalizedProjectPath)
   491→		return nil // Not an error, just doesn't match
   492→	}
   493→
   494→	slog.Info("processCodexSessionFile: Session matched project",
   495→		"sessionID", sessionID,
   496→		"sessionPath", sessionPath)
   497→
   498→	// Create session info
   499→	sessionInfo := &codexSessionInfo{
   500→		SessionID:   sessionID,
   501→		SessionPath: sessionPath,
   502→		Meta:        meta,
   503→	}
   504→
   505→	// Process the session
   506→	agentSession, err := processSessionToAgentChat(sessionInfo, projectPath, getWatcherDebugRaw())
   507→	if err != nil {
   508→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to process session: %w", err)
   509→	}
   510→
   511→	// Skip empty sessions
   512→	if agentSession == nil {
   513→		slog.Debug("processCodexSessionFile: Skipping empty session", "sessionID", sessionID)
   514→		return nil
   515→	}
   516→
   517→	// Skip sessions without user message (empty slug)
   518→	if agentSession.Slug == "" {
   519→		slog.Debug("processCodexSessionFile: Skipping session without user message",
   520→			"sessionID", agentSession.SessionID)
   521→		return nil
   522→	}
   523→
   524→	slog.Info("processCodexSessionFile: Calling callback for session", "sessionID", agentSession.SessionID)
   525→	// Call the callback in a goroutine to avoid blocking
   526→	go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   527→		defer func() {
   528→			if r := recover(); r != nil {
   529→				slog.Error("processCodexSessionFile: Callback panicked", "panic", r)
   530→			}
   531→		}()
   532→		callback(s)
   533→	}(agentSession)
   534→
   535→	return nil
   536→}
   537→
```

## Full Trace of WatchAgent Callback Invocation Flow

Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's the complete flow from fsnotify event to callback invocation:

### Architecture Overview

The callback is invoked **on every fsnotify event** (file write/create) with **NO deduplication or debouncing**. The system rereads the entire JSONL file on each modification and processes all sessions/records without tracking what was previously sent.

### Call Chain: fsnotify → Callback

#### Claude Code Provider Flow

**1. Interface Entry Point** (`pkg/spi/provider.go:97`)
```go
WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
```

**2. Provider Implementation** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/provider.go:430-480`)
```go
func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    wrappedCallback := func(agentChatSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
        sessionCallback(agentChatSession)  // Passes through to user callback
    }
    SetWatcherCallback(wrappedCallback)
    startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir)  // Starts background watcher
    <-ctx.Done()  // Blocks until cancellation
}
```

**3. File System Watcher** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/watcher.go:99-251`)
```go
func startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir string) error {
    go func() {  // Background goroutine
        watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
        watcher.Add(actualProjectDir)
        
        for {
            case event := <-watcher.Events:
                if event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
                    scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)  // Triggers on EVERY event
                }
        }
    }()
}
```

**4. File Scan and Session Processing** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/watcher.go:254-354`)
```go
func scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir string, changedFile ...string) {
    slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === START SCAN ===")
    
    // Create parser and reparse entire JSONL file
    parser := NewJSONLParser()
    
    // If changedFile provided, extract session ID from that file
    if targetFile != "" {
        targetSessionUuid, _ := extractSessionIDFromFile(targetFile)
        parser.ParseProjectSessionsForSession(claudeProjectDir, true, targetSessionUuid)
    } else {
        parser.ParseProjectSessions(claudeProjectDir, true)
    }
    
    // Get the callback function (thread-safe)
    callback := getWatcherCallback()
    
    // Process each session and CALL CALLBACK
    for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
        if len(session.Records) == 0 { continue }
        if targetSessionUuid != "" && session.SessionUuid != targetSessionUuid { continue }
        
        agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, "", getWatcherDebugRaw())
        if agentSession != nil {
            slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Calling callback for session", "sessionId", agentSession.SessionID)
            
            // CALLBACK INVOKED HERE - IN A GOROUTINE
            go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
                callback(s)
            }(agentSession)
        }
    }
    
    slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === END SCAN ===")
}
```

**5. Session Conversion** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/watcher.go:495-558`)
```go
func convertToAgentChatSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
    // Filter warmup messages
    filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
    
    // Skip if no real messages
    if len(filteredRecords) == 0 { return nil }
    
    // Generate SessionData from FILTERED records
    sessionData, _ := GenerateAgentSession(filteredSession, workspaceRoot)
    
    // Preserve RAW data with ALL records (including warmup)
    for _, record := range session.Records {
        rawDataBuilder.Write(json.Marshal(record.Data))
    }
    
    return &spi.AgentChatSession{
        SessionID: session.SessionUuid,
        SessionData: sessionData,  // Filtered
        RawData: rawDataBuilder.String(),  // All records
    }
}
```

#### Codex CLI Provider Flow

The Codex CLI provider follows the same pattern:

**Codex File Watch** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/watcher.go:156-379`)
```go
func startCodexSessionWatcher(projectPath string, sessionsRoot string, initialDayDir string) error {
    go func() {
        for {
            case event := <-watcher.Events:
                if strings.HasSuffix(eventPath, ".jsonl") {
                    ScanCodexSessions(projectPath, parentDir, &eventPath)  // Triggers scan
                }
        }
    }()
}
```

**Codex Scan** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/watcher.go:384-457`)
```go
func ScanCodexSessions(projectPath string, sessionDir string, changedFile *string) {
    slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: === START SCAN ===")
    
    callback := getWatcherCallback()
    
    if changedFile != nil {
        processCodexSessionFile(*changedFile, projectPath, ..., callback)  // Single file
    } else {
        // Scan all JSONL files in directory
        for _, entry := range os.ReadDir(sessionDir) {
            processCodexSessionFile(sessionPath, projectPath, ..., callback)
        }
    }
    
    slog.Info("ScanCodexSessions: === END SCAN ===")
}
```

**Codex File Processing** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/watcher.go:459-536`)
```go
func processCodexSessionFile(sessionPath string, projectPath string, ..., callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    // Load metadata
    meta, _ := loadCodexSessionMeta(sessionPath)
    
    // Check if matches project path
    matched := (normalizedCWD == normalizedProjectPath)
    if !matched { return nil }
    
    // Process entire session
    agentSession, _ := processSessionToAgentChat(&sessionInfo, projectPath, getWatcherDebugRaw())
    
    if agentSession != nil && agentSession.Slug != "" {
        slog.Info("processCodexSessionFile: Calling callback for session", "sessionID", agentSession.SessionID)
        
        // CALLBACK INVOKED HERE - IN A GOROUTINE
        go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
            callback(s)
        }(agentSession)
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

### Root Cause: Why Duplicates with Identical Counts?

Looking at the code structure, here are the reasons for duplicate callbacks with identical message counts:

1. **No Deduplication**: The system has **zero deduplication logic**. Every fsnotify event triggers a complete rescan of the entire JSONL file(s).

2. **Event Frequency**: Claude Code (and Codex) write JSONL files incrementally. Each append generates an fsnotify `Write` event. If the agent appends in quick succession:
   - Message sent → fsnotify Write event → callback invoked with full message
   - Agent processes internally → fsnotify Write event → callback invoked again
   - Each callback rereads the ENTIRE file and processes ALL messages to date

3. **No State Tracking**: The watcher has no mechanism to:
   - Track the last byte position read
   - Cache message counts
   - Skip previously-sent sessions
   - Debounce rapid file changes

4. **Multi-file Scanning**: Even with `changedFile` parameter, `scanJSONLFiles` still:
   - Parses the entire target file
   - Rereads all records from beginning
   - Regenerates SessionData each time
   - Calls callback unconditionally

5. **Goroutine Callbacks**: The callback runs in a spawned goroutine (`go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession)`), so:
   - Multiple callbacks can execute concurrently
   - No ordering guarantees
   - No synchronization between successive invocations

### Example Scenario

When Claude Code appends a user message to an active session:

```
Time 1: Write event → scanJSONLFiles() → reads 5 lines → callback(5 exchanges)
Time 2: 5ms later, Write event → scanJSONLFiles() → reads same 5 lines → callback(5 exchanges) ✓ DUPLICATE
Time 3: 10ms later, Write event → scanJSONLFiles() → reads 6 lines → callback(6 exchanges)
Time 4: Agent internal processing → Write event → reads 6 lines → callback(6 exchanges) ✓ DUPLICATE
```

Both calls at Time 2 and Time 4 send the same message counts because:
- They read the same lines from the file
- Both generate the same SessionData
- The provider has no record of what was already sent
- There's no comparison against previous invocations

### Multi-Provider Context

The `WatchAgents` function in `pkg/utils/watch_agents.go` wraps each provider's watcher in concurrent goroutines. This **multiplies** the duplicate issue:

```go
for providerID, provider := range providers {
    go func() {
        wrappedCallback := func(session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
            sessionCallback(providerID, session)  // User callback
        }
        provider.WatchAgent(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, wrappedCallback)
    }()
}
```

Each provider runs independently, potentially all firing callbacks at nearly the same time for the same file changes.

### Summary: The Callback Chain

1. **fsnotify event** (Write/Create on .jsonl)
2. → **scanJSONLFiles()** or **ScanCodexSessions()**
3. → **extractSessionIDFromFile()** or **loadCodexSessionMeta()**
4. → **parser.ParseProjectSessions()** (rereads entire file)
5. → **convertToAgentChatSession()** or **processSessionToAgentChat()** (reparses all records)
6. → **generateAgentSession()** (regenerates SessionData every time)
7. → **getWatcherCallback()** (retrieves callback function)
8. → **callback(agentSession)** spawned in goroutine
9. → **User's sessionCallback** receives full AgentChatSession with same message counts

**No state is persisted between invocations**, so identical file reads produce identical callbacks.

**Dots:** You're right, `...` (ellipsis) is the conventional truncation indicator. I'll fix `truncateSessionID` to use 3 dots.

**Agent msgs:** Yes, right now every `schema.RoleAgent` message gets counted — tool calls, tool results, text responses, internal processing steps — all lumped together. That makes the number noisy and hard to interpret.

Some options for a more meaningful metric:

1. **Count exchanges** — each exchange is roughly one conversational "turn" (user says something, agent responds). This would give a small, stable number that reflects conversation length. Label: `3 turns` or `3 exchanges`.

2. **Count only agent text messages** — filter to agent messages that have `Content` (text), skipping pure tool-call messages. Closer to "how many times did the agent speak." But still can be multiple per turn.

3. **Drop agent msgs entirely** — the prompt count already tells you conversation size. The markdown size tells you output volume. Agent message count may not add much value. Keep the output simpler.

4. **Count tool uses** — number of agent messages with a `Tool` field. Shows how much work the agent did. Label: `12 tool uses`.

I'd recommend **option 1 (count exchanges)** — it's the most intuitive "how long is this conversation" signal, stable (doesn't jump around within a turn), and complements the prompt count well. What do you think?

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

Extract key fields from all 180 JSON files

```bash
cd "/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/compositions/watch-2/.specstory/debug/019c7d4b-22b8-7b22-ad07-df6bcffdd205" && for i in $(seq 1 180); do echo "=== $i.json ===" && python3 -c "
import json, sys
with open('$i.json') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
typ = d.get('type', 'N/A')
pt = d.get('payload', {}).get('type', 'N/A') if isinstance(d.get('payload'), dict) else 'N/A'
pr = d.get('payload', {}).get('role', 'N/A') if isinstance(d.get('payload'), dict) else 'N/A'
payload = d.get('payload', {})
content = payload.get('content', []) if isinstance(payload, dict) else []
ct = content[0].get('type', 'N/A') if isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0 and isinstance(content[0], dict) else ('string' if isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0 and isinstance(content[0], str) else 'N/A')
has_name = 'yes' if isinstance(payload, dict) and 'name' in payload else 'no'
# Also check for tool_use content items
tool_types = []
if isinstance(content, list):
    for item in content:
        if isinstance(item, dict):
            tool_types.append(item.get('type','?'))
print(f'{typ}|{pt}|{pr}|{ct}|{has_name}|{\";\".join(tool_types)}')
" 2>&1; done
```

    83→// startProvenanceFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher that pushes FileEvents
    84→// to the provenance engine for correlation with agent activity. Returns a cleanup
    85→// function the caller must invoke (typically via defer). If the engine is nil
    86→// (provenance disabled), returns a no-op cleanup.
    87→func startProvenanceFSWatcher(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, rootDir string) (func(), error) {
    88→	if engine == nil {
    89→		return func() {}, nil
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	watcher, err := provenance.NewFSWatcher(engine, rootDir)
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance FS watcher: %w", err)
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	watcher.Start(ctx)
    98→	return watcher.Stop, nil
    99→}

    26→func WatchAgents(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession)) error {

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:03:56Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants me to compare the two implementations and identify the differences. Let me lay them out.

**`main.go` (lines 689-810) — the watch command's inline implementation:**
1. Uses `providerIDs` which can be filtered to a single provider via args (line 643-644)
2. Has the full session callback with: markdown processing, created/updated detection, message counting, human/JSON output, provenance events
3. Error handling: distinguishes context cancellation (expected on Ctrl+C) from real errors
4. Uses `errChan` and waits for all watchers, tracking `lastError`


5. Has access to all the watch command's local state: `config`, `useUTC`, `silent`, `jsonOutput`, `onlyCloudSync`, `provenanceEngine`, `debugRaw`, etc.

**`utils/WatchAgents` (watch_agents.go):**
1. Gets ALL providers from registry (no filtering)
2. Callback just wraps with providerID and passes through — no processing logic
3. Error handling: logs errors but continues, uses WaitGroup + done channel
4. Uses `select` on ctx.Done vs done channel
5. Has a nil check issue on line 61 where it accesses `session.SessionData.Exchanges` without verifying the session data exists first

The main differences come down to scope and safety: main.go filters to specific providers and does all the heavy lifting in its callback with markdown processing and output formatting, while WatchAgents watches everything and just passes data through. Error handling differs too — main.go expects context cancellation as normal, but WatchAgents surfaces it as an error. There's also a concurrency pattern difference with error channels versus WaitGroups, and critically, WatchAgents has that nil safety gap that could cause a panic.

Looking at whether they're actually duplicating work: the core concurrent watching pattern is definitely there in both, but main.go's callback does so much domain-specific logic that WatchAgents doesn't really provide a shortcut. I could theoretically refactor main.go to use WatchAgents with a richer callback, but that would require WatchAgents to support provider filtering, fix the nil checks, and handle context cancellation differently. The callback signature itself could work — WatchAgents accepts a callback that takes providerID and session, which is what main.go uses — but main.go's callback also needs the Provider object for processing, which WatchAgents doesn't pass along.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:04:03Z)**_

Here are the meaningful differences between the two:

| | `main.go` (inline) | `utils/WatchAgents` |
|---|---|---|
| **Provider filtering** | Supports `args[0]` to watch a single provider | Always watches all registered providers |
| **Callback signature** | `func(session)` — captures `p spi.Provider` from closure | `func(providerID, session)` — no provider object passed |
| **Callback body** | Does everything: markdown processing, output, provenance | Just forwards session data to caller |
| **Nil safety** | Checks `session == nil` (line 700) | Accesses `session.SessionData.Exchanges` on line 61 without nil check |
| **Context cancellation** | Treats it as expected (Ctrl+C), sends nil to errChan | Returns `ctx.Err()` which surfaces as an error |
| **Concurrency pattern** | errChan + counter | WaitGroup + done channel + select |

The core "launch a goroutine per provider, call `WatchAgent`, collect errors" pattern is duplicated. The main reason `main.go` can't use `WatchAgents` today is that `WatchAgents` doesn't pass the `spi.Provider` object to the callback — and `main.go` needs it for `processSingleSession(session, p, ...)`. It also doesn't support provider filtering.

These are fixable — `WatchAgents` could be extended to pass the provider object and accept an optional provider filter. But that's a refactor decision. The nil safety bug on line 61 of `WatchAgents` is worth fixing regardless.

_**User (2026-02-21 13:14:06Z)**_

OK, so the provider filtering and callback body are trivial to fix. That leaves just context cancellation and concurrency pattern as differences.

Explain the differences to me, and what is good/bad about each option, and which one we should pick for the shared WatchAgents

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:14:22Z)**_



**Context cancellation:**

- **`main.go`**: Treats `context.Canceled` / `context.DeadlineExceeded` as expected (Ctrl+C), sends `nil` to the error channel. Only real errors get surfaced. This means the caller sees a clean exit on Ctrl+C.
- **`WatchAgents`**: Returns `ctx.Err()` on cancellation, which surfaces as an error to the caller. The caller would need to know to ignore context cancellation errors themselves.

`main.go`'s approach is better. Ctrl+C is the normal exit path for a watch command — it shouldn't be reported as an error. The watcher should handle that internally so callers don't all need to filter it out.

**Concurrency pattern:**

- **`main.go`**: Uses `errChan` with a counter (`for i := 0; i < len(providerIDs)`). Waits for every goroutine to send exactly one value. Tracks `lastError` from the channel.
- **`WatchAgents`**: Uses `WaitGroup` + a separate `done` channel + `select` between `ctx.Done()` and `done`. On `ctx.Done()`, it also calls `wg.Wait()` before returning. Error channel is buffered but uses non-blocking send (can silently drop errors if the channel fills).

The `WaitGroup` approach is more idiomatic Go for "wait for N goroutines." But the `select` between `ctx.Done()` and `done` is redundant — once the context is cancelled, the providers' `WatchAgent` calls should return, which completes the WaitGroup anyway. And the non-blocking error send is a bug: if more providers error than the channel buffer size, errors get silently dropped.

**Recommendation**: Take `main.go`'s context cancellation handling (filter out cancellation errors inside the watcher, return nil for clean Ctrl+C exits). Use `WaitGroup` for waiting on goroutines (more idiomatic than a counter), but drop the `select`/`done` channel — just `wg.Wait()` directly. Use a blocking error channel send so errors are never silently dropped.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package utils

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"sync"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
)

// WatchAgents starts watchers for all registered providers concurrently
// Calls sessionCallback when any provider detects activity
// Continues watching even if individual providers error (logs errors but keeps running)
// Runs until context is cancelled or all watchers stop
//
// Parameters:
//   - ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
//   - projectPath: Agent's working directory to watch
//   - debugRaw: whether to write debug raw data files
//   - sessionCallback: called with provider ID and AgentChatSession data on each update
//
// The callback includes the provider ID to help consumers route/filter sessions.
// The callback should not block as it may delay other provider notifications.
func WatchAgents(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
	slog.Info("WatchAgents: Starting multi-provider watch", "projectPath", projectPath, "debugRaw", debugRaw)

	// Get all registered providers
	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
	providers := registry.GetAll()

	if len(providers) == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("no providers registered")
	}

	slog.Info("WatchAgents: Found providers", "count", len(providers))

	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	errChan := make(chan error, len(providers))

	// Start a watcher for each provider
	for providerID, provider := range providers {
		providerID := providerID // Capture loop variable
		provider := provider     // Capture loop variable

		wg.Add(1)
		go func() {
			defer wg.Done()

			slog.Info("WatchAgents: Starting watcher for provider", "providerID", providerID, "providerName", provider.Name())

			// Wrap the callback to include provider ID
			wrappedCallback := func(session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
				if session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
					return
				}

				slog.Debug("WatchAgents: Provider callback fired",
					"providerID", providerID,
					"sessionID", session.SessionID,
					"exchanges", len(session.SessionData.Exchanges))

				// Call the user's callback with provider ID
				sessionCallback(providerID, session)
			}

			err := provider.WatchAgent(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, wrappedCallback)
			if err != nil {
				// Context cancellation is expected when user presses Ctrl+C, not an error
				if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) {
					slog.Info("WatchAgents: Provider watcher stopped", "provider", provider.Name())
					errChan <- nil
				} else {
					slog.Error("WatchAgents: Provider watcher failed", "provider", provider.Name(), "error", err)
					errChan <- fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", provider.Name(), err)
				}
			} else {
				errChan <- nil
			}
		}()
	}

	// Wait for all watchers to complete (they run until Ctrl+C)
	wg.Wait()

	// Collect errors
	var lastError error
	for range len(providers) {
		if err := <-errChan; err != nil {
			lastError = err
			slog.Error("WatchAgents: Provider watch error", "error", err)
		}
	}

	return lastError
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:17:13Z)**_

All clean. Here's what changed in `watch_agents.go`:

- **Context cancellation**: Now filters out `context.Canceled` and `context.DeadlineExceeded` as expected Ctrl+C behavior (sends `nil` to error channel), matching `main.go`'s approach. Only real errors get surfaced.
- **Concurrency**: Replaced `WaitGroup` + `done` channel + `select` with `WaitGroup` + direct `wg.Wait()`. Removed the redundant `select` between `ctx.Done()` and `done`. 
- **Error collection**: Replaced the non-blocking send (which could silently drop errors) with a blocking send to `errChan`. After `wg.Wait()`, drains the channel and returns the last error, same pattern as `main.go`.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:20:24Z)**_



Five options:

1. **`WatchProviders`** — direct, says what it does. Generic enough.
2. **`WatchSpecificAgents`** — explicit about filtering, but wordy.
3. **`WatchAgentsWithProviders`** — clear relationship to `WatchAgents`, but long.
4. **`WatchSelectedAgents`** — implies a selection was made, reasonable.
5. **`RunAgentWatchers`** — different verb, avoids confusion with `WatchAgents`.

**Recommendation**: `WatchProviders`. It's the most direct — you're giving it a map of providers and it watches them. `WatchAgents` becomes the convenience wrapper that resolves "all registered providers" and calls `WatchProviders`. The naming naturally communicates the layering: `WatchAgents` = all agents, `WatchProviders` = specific ones you pass in.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package utils

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"sync"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
)

// WatchAgents starts watchers for all registered providers concurrently.
// Convenience wrapper around WatchProviders that resolves the full provider registry.
func WatchAgents(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
	providers := registry.GetAll()

	if len(providers) == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("no providers registered")
	}

	return WatchProviders(ctx, projectPath, providers, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
}

// WatchProviders starts watchers for the given providers concurrently.
// Calls sessionCallback when any provider detects activity.
// Runs until context is cancelled or all watchers stop.
// Context cancellation (Ctrl+C) is treated as a clean exit, not an error.
//
// Parameters:
//   - ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
//   - projectPath: Agent's working directory to watch
//   - providers: map of provider ID to provider instance to watch
//   - debugRaw: whether to write debug raw data files
//   - sessionCallback: called with provider ID and AgentChatSession data on each update
//
// The callback includes the provider ID to help consumers route/filter sessions.
// The callback should not block as it may delay other provider notifications.
func WatchProviders(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, providers map[string]spi.Provider, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
	slog.Info("WatchProviders: Starting multi-provider watch", "projectPath", projectPath, "providerCount", len(providers), "debugRaw", debugRaw)

	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	errChan := make(chan error, len(providers))

	for providerID, provider := range providers {
		providerID := providerID // Capture loop variable
		provider := provider     // Capture loop variable

		wg.Add(1)
		go func() {
			defer wg.Done()

			slog.Info("WatchProviders: Starting watcher for provider", "providerID", providerID, "providerName", provider.Name())

			// Wrap the callback to include provider ID
			wrappedCallback := func(session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
				if session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
					return
				}

				slog.Debug("WatchProviders: Provider callback fired",
					"providerID", providerID,
					"sessionID", session.SessionID,
					"exchanges", len(session.SessionData.Exchanges))

				sessionCallback(providerID, session)
			}

			err := provider.WatchAgent(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, wrappedCallback)
			if err != nil {
				// Context cancellation is expected when user presses Ctrl+C, not an error
				if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) {
					slog.Info("WatchProviders: Provider watcher stopped", "provider", provider.Name())
					errChan <- nil
				} else {
					slog.Error("WatchProviders: Provider watcher failed", "provider", provider.Name(), "error", err)
					errChan <- fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", provider.Name(), err)
				}
			} else {
				errChan <- nil
			}
		}()
	}

	// Wait for all watchers to complete (they run until Ctrl+C)
	wg.Wait()

	// Collect errors
	var lastError error
	for range len(providers) {
		if err := <-errChan; err != nil {
			lastError = err
			slog.Error("WatchProviders: Provider watch error", "error", err)
		}
	}

	return lastError
}

All clean. Here's what changed:

**`pkg/utils/watch_agents.go`**:
- Extracted `WatchProviders(ctx, projectPath, providers, debugRaw, sessionCallback)` — takes an explicit `map[string]spi.Provider` so callers control which providers to watch.
- `WatchAgents` is now a thin wrapper: resolves the registry, checks for empty, calls `WatchProviders`.

**`main.go`**:
- Replaced the entire inline goroutine-per-provider loop + errChan + error collection (~130 lines) with a single call: `return utils.WatchProviders(ctx, cwd, providers, debugRaw, sessionCallback)`.
- The session callback stays in `main.go` (it has the domain logic: markdown processing, output formatting, provenance).
- The concurrency, error handling, and context cancellation are now handled by `WatchProviders`.
- Removed unused `errors` import.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:49:39Z)**_

So `ExecAgentAndWatch` is a fundamentally different operation — it exec's the agent process, blocks until it exits, and calls the callback as the agent runs. `WatchAgent` is passive monitoring only.

The `run` command can't use `WatchProviders` because:

1. It calls `ExecAgentAndWatch` (exec + watch), not `WatchAgent` (watch only)
2. It's always a single provider, not concurrent multi-provider
3. It blocks on the agent process, not on context cancellation
4. The callback has different concerns — it tracks `lastRunSessionID` for deep linking on exit, and doesn't produce any stdout output

The callback logic (calling `processSingleSession` and `processProvenanceEvents`) is shared, but the orchestration is completely different. There's not really a DRY violation here — `run` and `watch` are different operations that happen to share the same "process a session update" callback body.

The only duplication is the callback internals (process session, handle errors, push provenance events). If that pattern grows, it could be extracted into a helper, but right now it's ~15 lines in each place and the two callbacks have different details (`run` tracks `lastRunSessionID`, `watch` does output formatting). Forcing them through `WatchProviders` would mean shoehorning `ExecAgentAndWatch` into a `WatchAgent` shape, which it isn't.

OK, so `AgentChatSession` has:
- `SessionID` (string)
- `CreatedAt` (string)
- `Slug` (string)
- `SessionData` (*schema.SessionData) - complex nested struct with exchanges, messages, etc.
- `RawData` (string) - the raw JSONL/JSON content

For deep equality:
- Go's `==` won't work because `SessionData` is a pointer to a struct containing slices and maps
- `reflect.DeepEqual` would work but it's expensive, especially on `RawData` which is a large string


- Comparing `RawData` directly is problematic since it grows with each JSONL append, even if the parsed `SessionData` hasn't actually changed. The real question is whether the message counts shifted — if they're identical, the session content is effectively the same. So I could track just the message counts per session and only trigger the callback when those change.

But actually, the simplest solution is what the user suggested: cache the last `AgentChatSession` for each session ID and compare against it directly. The user wants the dedup logic in `WatchProviders` so API consumers benefit too. I'm weighing the tradeoffs: `reflect.DeepEqual` on `SessionData` is accurate and catches any structural change, and since we're already doing expensive operations like full JSONL parsing and markdown generation per callback, the cost is negligible. Message count comparison would be faster but could miss changes to existing messages, while comparing `RawData` would catch everything but isn't the right level of abstraction.

`reflect.DeepEqual` handles the nested structures correctly—maps, slices, and pointers all work as expected—so it's the right semantic check for whether the structured session data has actually changed.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 13:56:12Z)**_

`AgentChatSession` does NOT have good equality semantics for Go's `==` operator. `SessionData` is a pointer to a struct containing slices and maps — `==` won't compile on it.

Options for comparison:

1. **`reflect.DeepEqual` on `SessionData`** — semantically correct ("has the structured data changed?"). Handles the nested slices, maps, and pointers in `SessionData`. Cost: struct traversal, but trivial compared to the JSONL parsing and markdown generation we already do per callback.

2. **Compare total message count** — very cheap. Track `(userPrompts, agentActivity)` per session ID. But misses changes within existing messages (content growing, metadata updating). In practice messages are append-only so this likely works, but it's a lossy proxy.

3. **Compare `RawData` string** — would catch every change, but that's the wrong signal. Raw data changes on every JSONL append even when `SessionData` doesn't change. That's literally the problem.

**Recommendation**: `reflect.DeepEqual` on `SessionData` in `WatchProviders`. It's the right semantic — "has the structured session data that drives markdown and output actually changed?" — and it's accurate without being expensive relative to what we already do. We'd hold a `map[string]*schema.SessionData` (keyed by session ID) and compare before forwarding the callback. This dedupes for all consumers, not just the CLI output.

One caveat: needs a `sync.Mutex` on the map since callbacks fire from goroutines.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-21 14:01:05Z)**_



Yes, exactly. A `map[string]int` keyed by session ID, holding the last-seen total message count. If the new count matches, skip the callback. The map lives in `WatchProviders` and is shared across the goroutines for that provider set, so it needs a `sync.Mutex`.

    17→	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
    18→	providers := registry.GetAll()
    19→
    20→	if len(providers) == 0 {
    21→		return fmt.Errorf("no providers registered")
    22→	}
    23→
    24→	return WatchProviders(ctx, projectPath, providers, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
    25→}
    26→
    27→// WatchProviders starts watchers for the given providers concurrently.
    28→// Calls sessionCallback when any provider detects activity.
    29→// Runs until context is cancelled or all watchers stop.
    30→// Context cancellation (Ctrl+C) is treated as a clean exit, not an error.
    31→//
    32→// Parameters:
    33→//   - ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
    34→//   - projectPath: Agent's working directory to watch
    35→//   - providers: map of provider ID to provider instance to watch
    36→//   - debugRaw: whether to write debug raw data files
    37→//   - sessionCallback: called with provider ID and AgentChatSession data on each update
    38→//
    39→// The callback includes the provider ID to help consumers route/filter sessions.
    40→// The callback should not block as it may delay other provider notifications.
    41→func WatchProviders(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, providers map[string]spi.Provider, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    42→	slog.Info("WatchProviders: Starting multi-provider watch", "projectPath", projectPath, "providerCount", len(providers), "debugRaw", debugRaw)
    43→
    44→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    45→	errChan := make(chan error, len(providers))
    46→
    47→	for providerID, provider := range providers {
    48→		providerID := providerID // Capture loop variable
    49→		provider := provider     // Capture loop variable
    50→
    51→		wg.Add(1)
    52→		go func() {
    53→			defer wg.Done()
    54→
    55→			slog.Info("WatchProviders: Starting watcher for provider", "providerID", providerID, "providerName", provider.Name())
    56→
    57→			// Wrap the callback to include provider ID
    58→			wrappedCallback := func(session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
    59→				if session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
    60→					return
    61→				}
    62→
    63→				slog.Debug("WatchProviders: Provider callback fired",
    64→					"providerID", providerID,
    65→					"sessionID", session.SessionID,
    66→					"exchanges", len(session.SessionData.Exchanges))
    67→
    68→				sessionCallback(providerID, session)
    69→			}
    70→
    71→			err := provider.WatchAgent(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, wrappedCallback)
    72→			if err != nil {
    73→				// Context cancellation is expected when user presses Ctrl+C, not an error
    74→				if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) {
    75→					slog.Info("WatchProviders: Provider watcher stopped", "provider", provider.Name())
    76→					errChan <- nil
    77→				} else {
    78→					slog.Error("WatchProviders: Provider watcher failed", "provider", provider.Name(), "error", err)
    79→					errChan <- fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", provider.Name(), err)
    80→				}
    81→			} else {
    82→				errChan <- nil
    83→			}
    84→		}()
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// Wait for all watchers to complete (they run until Ctrl+C)
    88→	wg.Wait()
    89→
    90→	// Collect errors
    91→	var lastError error
    92→	for range len(providers) {
    93→		if err := <-errChan; err != nil {
    94→			lastError = err
    95→			slog.Error("WatchProviders: Provider watch error", "error", err)
    96→		}
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	return lastError
   100→}
   101→
```
</details></tool-use>
